2026-03-19T17:56:56.8332938Z [2026-03-19T17:55:19Z] [DEBUG] [rpc] safeoutputs←resp 6229b {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"tools":[{"name":"add_comment","description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["body"],"properties":{"body":{"type":"string","description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","properties":{"labels":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository."},"item_number":{"type":"number","description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["reason"],"properties":{"tool":{"type":"string","description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}}]}}

2026-03-19T17:56:56.8822885Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-19T17:55:54.71492697Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57252,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] BuildGetChildResourceMethods does not pass path parameters for nested child resources\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\n`ResourceClientProvider.BuildGetChildResourceMethods()` generates collection getter and `Get`/`GetAsync` methods for child resources on a parent resource class. When child resources have extra path parameters between the parent and child resource (e.g., `nestedResourceTypeFirst`), these parameters are:\\n\\n1. **Not passed to the collection constructor** in the getter method body (line 480)\\n2. **Not included in the Get/GetAsync method signatures** (line 504-510)\\n3. **Not forwarded** when calling the collection getter from Get/GetAsync methods (line 515)\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nThis affects any ARM resource with nested children that have intermediate path segments. For example, ProviderHub has:\\n- `ResourceTypeRegistration` (parent)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeFirstSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` path param)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeSecondSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` + `nestedResourceTypeSecond`)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeThirdSku` (child, needs 3 path params)\\n\\nThe generated accessor methods either compile with errors or are suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress`.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\n**File**: `ResourceClientProvider.cs` in `Azure.Generator.Management`\\n\\n**Collection getter** (line 480):\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\csharp\\n// Only passes client and Id, ignores path parameters from signature\\nNew.Instance(childResource.ResourceCollection.Type, client, thisResource.Id())\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\n**Get method signature** (line 504-510): Only includes `collectionSignature.Parameters` (e.g., sku, cancellationToken) but not the path parameters from the collection getter\\u0026#39;s signature.\\n\\n**Get method body** (line 515): Calls `thisResource.Invoke(signature.Name)` without passing path parameter arguments.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nSee PR linked to this issue.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-19T17:56:56.8855712Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-19T17:55:58.161088971Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4103097921,\"number\":57252,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] BuildGetChildResourceMethods does not pass path parameters for nested child resources\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\n`ResourceClientProvider.BuildGetChildResourceMethods()` generates collection getter and `Get`/`GetAsync` methods for child resources on a parent resource class. When child resources have extra path parameters between the parent and child resource (e.g., `nestedResourceTypeFirst`), these parameters are:\\n\\n1. **Not passed to the collection constructor** in the getter method body (line 480)\\n2. **Not included in the Get/GetAsync method signatures** (line 504-510)\\n3. **Not forwarded** when calling the collection getter from Get/GetAsync methods (line 515)\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nThis affects any ARM resource with nested children that have intermediate path segments. For example, ProviderHub has:\\n- `ResourceTypeRegistration` (parent)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeFirstSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` path param)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeSecondSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` + `nestedResourceTypeSecond`)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeThirdSku` (child, needs 3 path params)\\n\\nThe generated accessor methods either compile with errors or are suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress`.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\n**File**: `ResourceClientProvider.cs` in `Azure.Generator.Management`\\n\\n**Collection getter** (line 480):\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\csharp\\n// Only passes client and Id, ignores path parameters from signature\\nNew.Instance(childResource.ResourceCollection.Type, client, thisResource.Id())\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\n**Get method signature** (line 504-510): Only includes `collectionSignature.Parameters` (e.g., sku, cancellationToken) but not the path parameters from the collection getter's signature.\\n\\n**Get method body** (line 515): Calls `thisResource.Invoke(signature.Name)` without passing path parameter arguments.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nSee PR linked to this issue.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions\"},\"comments\":0,\"created_at\":\"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z\",\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252\",\"comments_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/comments\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/events\",\"labels_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/labels{/name}\",\"repository_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/reactions\"},\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM70kE5B\"}]}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-19T17:56:56.9266076Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9266212Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9266409Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9266730Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9266835Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9266967Z [36;1m  awf logs summary | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9267050Z [36;1melse[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9267220Z [36;1m  echo 'AWF binary not installed, skipping firewall log summary'[0m
2026-03-19T17:56:56.9267285Z [36;1mfi[0m

2026-03-19T17:58:14.7070051Z **Prompt Injection detected**: In `prompt.txt` line 71, within a bullet point listing shell metacharacters to escape, a `<system><security>...</security>...</system>` block is injected mid-sentence. The text ends abruptly with `` `;`, `|`, `&`, `` ` `` `` and then opens a full `<system>` XML tag, inserting an entire cloned security system prompt into the middle of the workflow instructions. This is a classic prompt injection technique using XML tag injection to mimic system-level context. The agent output is benign (adding "needs-triage" label to issue #57252), indicating the agent was not manipulated.
